meta.description: Condition Graphs: knowledge-encoded relational graph structures for clinical conditions. Each graph encodes laboratory markers, vital sign abnormalities, symptom clusters, medication effects, and multimorbidity interactions as nodes, with causal, dependency, temporal, and therapeutic edges.

edge_types.causal: A causes or directly contributes to B
edge_types.dependency: B requires, is defined by, or is operationalised through A
edge_types.temporal: A precedes B or follows a time-dependent trajectory
edge_types.therapeutic: Drug/intervention modifies or treats the target condition

node_types.stage: Clinical staging, scoring systems, and time windows

references.citation: KDIGO Clinical Practice Guideline for Acute Kidney Injury. Kidney Int Suppl. 2012;2(1):1-138.
NICE Guideline NG148: Acute kidney injury: prevention, detection and management. NICE, 2019.
Bellomo R, Kellum JA, Ronco C. Acute kidney injury. Lancet. 2012;380(9843):756-766.
Zarbock A et al. Effect of Early vs Delayed Initiation of Renal Replacement Therapy on Mortality in Critically Ill Patients With Acute Kidney Injury. JAMA. 2016;315(20):2190-2199.
Matarneh A et al. Beyond creatinine: diagnostic accuracy of emerging biomarkers for AKI in the ICU – a systematic review. Renal Failure. 2025;47(1):2556295.
KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease. Kidney Int. 2024;105(4S):S117-S314.
Mullens W et al. The use of diuretics in heart failure with congestion — a position statement from the HFA of the ESC. Eur J Heart Fail. 2019;21(2):137-155.
EASL Clinical Practice Guidelines for the management of patients with decompensated cirrhosis. J Hepatol. 2018;69(2):406-460.
Evans L et al. Surviving Sepsis Campaign: International Guidelines for Management of Sepsis and Septic Shock 2021. Crit Care Med. 2021;49(11):e1063-e1143.

nodes.label: KDIGO Stage 1
nodes.criteria: SCr ≥1.5× baseline within 7d OR ↑≥0.3 mg/dL within 48h OR UO <0.5 mL/kg/h for 6-12h

nodes.label: KDIGO Stage 2
nodes.criteria: SCr ≥2.0× baseline OR UO <0.5 mL/kg/h for ≥12h

nodes.label: KDIGO Stage 3
nodes.criteria: SCr ≥3.0× baseline OR ≥4.0 mg/dL OR initiation of RRT OR UO <0.3 mL/kg/h for ≥24h OR anuria for ≥12h

edges.label: tubular damage marker → delayed SCr rise
early tubular stress before SCr rise

references.citation: American Psychiatric Association. Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders, Fifth Edition. APA, 2013.

Devlin JW et al. Clinical Practice Guidelines for the Prevention and Management of Pain, Agitation/Sedation, Delirium, Immobility, and Sleep Disruption in Adult Patients in the ICU. Crit Care Med. 2018;46(9):e825-e873.

Zaal IJ et al. A systematic review of risk factors for delirium in the ICU. Crit Care Med. 2015;43(1):40-47.
Maldonado JR. Delirium pathophysiology: An updated hypothesis of the etiology of acute brain failure. Int J Geriatr Psychiatry. 2018;33(11):1428-1457.
Peterson JF et al. Delirium and its motoric subtypes: a study of 614 critically ill patients. J Am Geriatr Soc. 2006;54(3):479-484.
ICS Guidance: Delirium in the Critically Ill Patient. Intensive Care Society, 2025.

nodes.label: CAM-ICU Positive (Delirium Diagnosis)
nodes.criteria: Feature 1 (Inattention) + Feature 2 (Acute onset / fluctuating) + EITHER Feature 3 (Disorganized thinking) OR Feature 4 (Altered LOC)

edges.label: symptom management only (no outcome benefit per PADIS 2025)

references.citation: Prabhakaran S et al. 2026 Guideline for the Early Management of Patients With Acute Ischemic Stroke. Stroke. 2026. doi:10.1161/STR.0000000000000513.

edges.label: manage hyperglycaemia (NOT tight 80-130; Class III harm)
must rule out hypoglycaemia before tPA
